decks.notes: This deck focuses on quickly assembling the Allosaurus Rider combo by using Eldritch Evolution and Neoform to tutor for key creatures such as Griselbrand and Xenagos, God of Revels. Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer protect the combo from disruption. Generous Ent and Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant provide additional threats, while Planar Genesis and Consign to Memory offer utility and card advantage. The deck aims to win the game by resolving the Allosaurus Rider combo and overwhelming the opponent with a powerful board presence.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly with cards like Manamorphose, Ruby Medallion, and Pyretic Ritual to cast multiple spells in a turn. It aims to assemble the Ruby Storm combo by chaining together spells and generating a large storm count, then finishing off the opponent with a lethal Grapeshot. The deck also includes cards like Reckless Impulse and Ral, Monsoon Mage to refill the hand and maximize spell potential. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells for a decisive victory.

The deck focuses on quickly establishing a board presence using cheap artifacts and leveraging the affinity mechanic to power out threats like Arcbound Ravager and Pinnacle Emissary. By deploying a mix of artifact creatures, equipment, and utility cards, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, aggressive plays. With cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga providing ramp and card advantage, the deck can quickly assemble a threatening board state and put pressure on the opponent while controlling the game with cards like Engineered Explosives and Sink into Stupor.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifact creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. By combining the speed of artifacts like Mishra's Bauble and Thundertrap Trainer with powerful spells like Unholy Heat and Lightning Bolt, the goal is to deal damage quickly and efficiently. The inclusion of counter spells like Spell Snare and Counterspell helps to protect the player's board and maintain control of the game. The mana base is carefully crafted to ensure consistency and flexibility in casting spells.

The focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells while maintaining board control and tempo. The key strategy is to play powerful creatures like Phlage and Ajani early on, backed up by cards like Ragavan and Seasoned Pyromancer to apply pressure. The deck also includes removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm to deal with threats from the opponent. Additionally, cards like Voice of Victory and Guide of Souls provide utility and card advantage. Overall, the Boros Aggro deck aims to quickly close out games with a combination of creatures and burn spells.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. It aims to control the board with cards like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah, while also using cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to deal damage efficiently. The deck also includes cards like Goblin Bombardment and Lightning Bolt for extra reach and removal. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to keep up the pressure and force the opponent to react quickly to avoid being overrun.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by ramping up mana quickly with cards like Utopia Sprawl and Talisman of Impulse, while also using Eldrazi Temple to bring out powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths. The deck also includes Kozilek's Command and Kozilek's Return for removal and disruption, as well as Malevolent Rumble for board control. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with big threats and control the game until victory is secured.

The deck focuses on using creatures with abilities that synergize well with each other, allowing for a toolbox-style approach to the game. Cards like Yawgmoth, Thran Physician and Agatha's Soul Cauldron provide value by sacrificing creatures, while Green Sun's Zenith and Boseiju, Who Endures help search for key pieces. Malevolent Rumble and Walking Ballista provide removal and finishing power, while cards like Dredger's Insight and Strangleroot Geist offer resilience and resilience against removal. Overall, the deck aims to create a strong board presence and outvalue the opponent through strategic creature interactions.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Cascade mechanic to cast Living End, a powerful sorcery that allows the player to bring back all creatures from their graveyard to the battlefield. By cycling through cards like Street Wraith and Endurance, the player can fill up their graveyard quickly and set up for a huge tempo swing with Living End. Additionally, cards like Force of Negation and Subtlety provide disruption to control the board while Generous Ent and Overlord of the Balemurk offer additional value and threat. The deck is designed to overwhelm the opponent with a massive graveyard recursion strategy.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on fast-paced, aggressive gameplay with a mix of efficient creatures and burn spells. The deck aims to quickly put pressure on the opponent by playing threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah early in the game. The inclusion of cards like Galvanic Discharge and Lightning Bolt allows for additional direct damage to finish off the opponent. The deck also utilizes cards like Arena of Glory and Guide of Souls to protect its creatures and ensure they can continue to attack.

This deck is focused on dealing direct damage to the opponent as quickly as possible. It uses efficient burn spells like Lightning Bolt, Rift Bolt, and Lava Spike along with aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear, Goblin Guide, and Chandra's Incinerator to chip away at the opponent's life total. The deck also includes cards like Skullcrack to prevent the opponent from gaining life and maintain pressure. The land base is designed to provide consistent access to red mana sources for casting spells and activating abilities.

The main idea of this deck is to play powerful red spells, generate a lot of mana with cards like Pyretic Ritual and Ruby Medallion, and then cast Past in Flames to replay all those spells from the graveyard. The deck aims to set up a big turn where it can chain together multiple spells with Ral, Monsoon Mage to deal a large amount of damage to the opponent in a single turn. The combo potential is enhanced with cards like Wish and Glimpse the Impossible, allowing for explosive plays and overwhelming the opponent quickly.

The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's strategy through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card advantage. By using cards like Counterspell, Fatal Push, and Sheoldred's Edict, the deck aims to maintain control of the game while also utilizing card draw spells like Consult the Star Charts and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian to accumulate resources. The deck also includes land destruction with Field of Ruin and focuses on maintaining a strong mana base with fetch lands like Polluted Delta and Flooded Strand.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quick and aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge for additional damage output. Blood Moon disrupts opponents' mana base while Seasoned Pyromancer generates card advantage. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury provide powerful finishers. The deck also includes support cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Elegant Parlor for added utility. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain a strong presence on the battlefield.

This deck focuses on aggressive gameplay with a mix of colors to maximize efficiency. It utilizes Leylines and binding effects to control the board and protect its key creatures like Phlage, Scion of Draco, and Ragavan. The addition of Lightning Bolt and Stubborn Denial provides versatile removal and counterspell options. The mana base allows for flexibility in casting spells, while Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and Consign to Memory offer additional utility and card advantage. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly with powerful creatures and disruptive spells.

The deck focuses on utilizing Emry, Lurker of the Loch and various artifacts like Mishra's Bauble, Mox Amber, and Mox Opal to generate a high amount of mana and card advantage. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces like Malevolent Rumble and Song of Creation to draw through the deck and win the game with Jace, Wielder of Mysteries. The deck also includes control elements like Repeal and Endurance to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its win condition.
